prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
### Echoes of Completion

Through shadowed valleys of doubt and grind,  
I trudged with burdens heavy as stone,  
Nights bleeding into dawns unkind,  
Threads of will frayed, yet never alone.  

The final weave, the last unyielding stitch,  
Unravels the storm within my core—  
A hush descends, soft as a switched-off switch,  
Relief floods in, an unlocking door.  

Exhaustion lingers like fading ink,  
But pride ignites, a quiet, fierce fire,  
The project yields its hard-won link  
To dreams once distant, now entire.
